name: Pork Tenderloin with Apples in Cider Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the pork tenderloin into medallions and flatten slightly by hand.
2. Season both sides of the medallions with salt and pepper.
3. Heat oil in a skillet over moderate heat.
4. Add the medallions to the skillet and brown on all sides, then remove them to a platter.
5. In the same skillet, melt butter over medium heat.
6. Add onions and sauté for 3-4 minutes until softened.
7. Add garlic and cook for another minute until translucent.
8. Add apple slices and turn the medallions in the sauce for about 2 minutes, then return them to the skillet and sprinkle with sage and thyme.
9. Pour in the cider and wine mixture, bring to a boil, reduce heat, and simmer for 5 minutes.
10. Continue cooking for another 5 minutes or until tender.
11. Remove pork and apples from the skillet using a slotted spoon and transfer to plates.
12. Raise the heat to high and reduce the cooking liquid to a light syrup consistency.
13. Pour over the medallions, season with salt and pepper, and serve hot.
